title: Purely Malty Tunisian Lamb Tagine
description: A traditional Tunisian dish featuring lamb, raisins, and confit tomatoes, perfect for a hearty dinner, and a great hangover cure due to its springy and malty flavor profile.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in the bottom of a clay or ceramic tagine or a large Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the lamb pieces and cook until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Add the malt extract, cumin, coriander, salt, and pepper, and stir to combine.
Add the raisins, confit tomatoes, dried apricots, chicken broth, and tomato paste, and stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 2 1/2 hours.
Serve the tagine hot, garnished with fresh parsley or cilantro, if desired.
Traditionally, the tagine is served with crusty bread or over couscous.
Let it rest for 10 minutes before serving to allow the flavors to meld together.
Cook poultry to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F before serving.
